---
title: "GSE164378 Robust Spike-Bulk Fit"
output: html_document
---

This diagnostic page uses the package spike fit directly. The bulk scale is
anchored by the MP median, while the spike boundary is proposed by a robust
MAD-standardized log-eigengap rule.

```{r setup}
library(scScale)
data(gse164378_3p_citeseq_hvg)

counts <- gse164378_3p_citeseq_hvg$rna_counts
count_transform <- "log1p_cpm"
```

## Fit

```{r fit}
fit <- scscale_fit(
  counts,
  count_transform = count_transform,
  fit_umi = FALSE,
  mp_max_iter = 300,
  mp_grid_n = 4000
)

data.frame(
  p = fit$p,
  n = fit$n,
  c_X = fit$c_X,
  method = fit$bulk$method,
  selected_K = sum(fit$spikes$is_spike),
  stop_reason = fit$bulk$stop_reason,
  tau2 = fit$bulk$tau2,
  lambda_plus = fit$bulk$lambda_plus,
  selected_gap_z = fit$bulk$history$gap_z[fit$bulk$selected_iteration],
  central_qq_loss = fit$bulk$history$central_qq_loss[fit$bulk$selected_iteration]
)

fit$bulk$history[fit$bulk$history$K %in% c(1:80, 200:230, fit$bulk$selected_iteration), c(
  "K", "n_bulk", "n_spikes", "tau2", "lambda_plus",
  "log_gap", "background_gap_median", "background_gap_mad",
  "gap_z", "central_qq_loss"
)]
```

## Diagnostic Plots

```{r plots, fig.width=11, fig.height=8}
lambda <- fit$spectrum$lambda
bulk <- fit$bulk
bulk_lambda <- lambda[bulk$bulk_index]
bulk_lambda <- bulk_lambda[is.finite(bulk_lambda) & bulk_lambda > 0]
spike_lambda <- lambda[bulk$spike_index]
spike_lambda <- spike_lambda[is.finite(spike_lambda) & spike_lambda > 0]
mp_density <- getFromNamespace("scscale_mp_density", "scScale")
mp_quantile <- getFromNamespace("scscale_mp_quantile_grid", "scScale")

mp_x <- seq(bulk$mp_lower, bulk$lambda_plus, length.out = 700)
mp_y <- mp_density(mp_x, c_X = fit$c_X, tau2 = bulk$tau2)

old_par <- par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4.2, 4.2, 2.6, 1))

plot(
  seq_along(lambda),
  lambda,
  log = "y",
  pch = 16,
  cex = 0.35,
  col = ifelse(seq_along(lambda) %in% bulk$spike_index, "#D55E00", "#0072B2"),
  xlab = "Eigenvalue rank",
  ylab = expression(lambda),
  main = paste0("Log spectrum, K=", length(bulk$spike_index))
)
abline(v = length(bulk$spike_index) + 0.5, col = "grey35", lty = 2)
abline(h = bulk$lambda_plus, col = "#D55E00", lty = 2)

emp_q <- sort(bulk_lambda)
theory_q <- mp_quantile(c_X = fit$c_X, prob = ppoints(length(emp_q)), tau2 = bulk$tau2, grid_n = 4000)
plot(
  theory_q,
  emp_q,
  pch = 16,
  cex = 0.35,
  col = "#0072B2",
  xlab = "MP theoretical quantile",
  ylab = "Empirical bulk quantile",
  main = "Bulk QQ"
)
abline(0, 1, col = "#D55E00", lwd = 2)

hist(
  bulk_lambda,
  breaks = 60,
  freq = FALSE,
  col = "grey85",
  border = "white",
  xlab = expression(lambda),
  main = "Bulk eigenvalues"
)
lines(mp_x, mp_y, col = "#D55E00", lwd = 2)
abline(v = bulk$lambda_plus, col = "#D55E00", lty = 2)

hist(
  log10(bulk_lambda),
  breaks = 60,
  freq = FALSE,
  col = "grey85",
  border = "white",
  xlab = expression(log[10](lambda)),
  main = "Log bulk eigenvalues"
)
abline(v = log10(bulk$lambda_plus), col = "#D55E00", lty = 2)
rug(log10(spike_lambda), col = "#D55E00", lwd = 1)

par(old_par)
```
